name: Gamilaraay

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Yuwaalaraay-Gamilaraay (gami1243).

notes: Pama-Nyungan (Yuwaalaraay-Gamilaraay) language of New South Wales, Australia. The single stop series is spelled with voiced letters (b d g, dental dh, palatal dj); rr is the alveolar trill and single r the retroflex approximant. Long vowels are written by doubling (aa ii uu). Word-initial stress and any predictable stop voicing/allophony are not encoded. The dh/dj values follow standard Australianist digraph conventions and some Gamilaraay materials instead spell the stops p/t/k; the language has no separate retroflex stop/nasal/lateral series.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Yuwaalaraay-Gamilaraay (gami1243)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
